figures.caption: Fig. 1: Multispectral optical (false RGB: infrared-red-green channels) image on the left and DEM height map on the right, both from the Hunan image set [9]. While the multispectral image highlights surface reflectance and land-cover differences, the DEM height map reveals the terrain structure, providing complementary information for scene interpretation.
TABLE IV: COMPARISON OF OUR PROPOSED MODEL WITH BASELINE ON DSTL TEST SET FOR CROP CLASS
TABLE V: COMPARISON OF OUR PROPOSED MODEL WITH BASELINE ON POTSDAM TEST SET FOR TREE CLASS

Fig. 3: Framework of the proposed CBC-SLP model that learns structured multimodal feature representations for robust semantic segmentation under missing or full modalities. CBC-SLP is based on a structured latent projection design that explicitly models shared and modality-specific representations before decoding.

Fig. 6: Qualitative semantic segmentation comparison on the Hunan image set under different modality-availability settings. From left to right: (a) SAR image, (b) MSI image, (c) DEM image, (d) ground truth, (e) CBC-SLP (ours), (f) CBC (baseline), (g) CMX, (h) M3L. Row 1 uses all modalities; Row 2 excludes MSI; Row 3 uses only DEM.
TABLE VII: DSTL TEST SET RESULTS UNDER SEPARATE AND UNIFIED STRATEGIES
TABLE VIII: POTSDAM TEST SET RESULTS UNDER SEPARATE AND UNIFIED STRATEGIES
TABLE VI: COMPARISON OF OUR PROPOSED MODEL WITH BASELINE ON HUNAN TEST SET FOR FOREST CLASS

Fig. 4: Qualitative semantic segmentation comparison on the DSTL image set under different modality-availability settings. From left to right: (a) RGB image, (b) NIR image, (c) SWIR image, (d) ground truth, (e) CBC-SLP (ours), (f) CBC (baseline), (g) CMX, (h) M3L. Row 1 uses all modalities; Row 2 uses only SWIR; Row 3 excludes NIR.

Fig. 5: Qualitative semantic segmentation comparison on the Potsdam image set under different modality-availability settings. From left to right: (a) RGB image, (b) IRRG image, (c) DSM image, (d) ground truth, (e) CBC-SLP (ours), (f) CBC (baseline), (g) CMX, (h) M3L. Row 1 uses all modalities; Row 2 uses only DSM; Row 3 excludes IRRG.
